source_window: Latest available arXiv new listings shown for Friday 2026-06-26; run invoked Sunday 2026-06-28 America/Chicago, with no newer weekend announcement visible.
summary: The selected papers emphasize finite-density QCD equation-of-state workflows, baryon stopping through rapidity-even directed-flow splitting, lattice-benchmarked hot-QCD meson screening, and physics ML methods for interpretability, reinforcement-learning parameter search, and multimodal black-box optimization. The strongest heavy-ion contribution is the MUSES/Calliope workflow paper, while the most broadly transferable AI/ML contribution is the interpretability/explainability review. One small-system spectrum-fit paper is flagged for caution because its QGP and transport interpretation appears stronger than the evidence visible at abstract level.
strongest_paper_summary: arXiv:2606.26326, the MUSES/Calliope calculation-engine paper, appears strongest for heavy-ion work because it addresses finite-density equation-of-state construction, transport quantities, hydrodynamic input generation, and BES-energy demonstration workflows in a reusable framework.

papers.title: Studying the QCD Matter produced in Heavy-Ion Collisions using the MUSES Calculation Engine
papers.authors_short: Johannes Jahan, Kevin P. Pala, Yumu Yang, Isabella Danhoni, et al.
papers.abstract: The paper presents the Calliope version of the MUSES Calculation Engine for hot and dense QCD matter, covering finite-density equations of state, strangeness and charge chemical potentials, thermodynamic derivatives, transport coefficients, EoS merging, inversion for hydrodynamic simulations, and BES-energy demonstrations.

papers.title: Axial-Vector Lattice Benchmarks Reveal a Common Medium Response of Meson Screening in Hot QCD
papers.authors_short: Jie Ren, Chen Chen, Fei Gao, Si-xue Qin
papers.abstract: The paper combines lattice-QCD benchmarks with a symmetry-preserving Dyson-Schwinger baseline for axial-vector and vector meson screening masses in hot QCD, then predicts light-charm and bottom-containing spectra.

papers.title: Rapidity-even directed flow splitting of protons and antiprotons as a probe of baryon stopping in relativistic heavy-ion collisions
papers.abstract: The paper uses 3+1D viscous hydrodynamics coupled to hadronic transport to show that rapidity-even directed-flow splitting between protons and antiprotons, especially the midrapidity curvature, can discriminate baryon-stopping profiles and baryon diffusion in BES-energy Au+Au collisions.

papers.title: Supercool with PPO: Exploring Supercooled Phase Transitions via Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors_short: Wan-Zhe Feng, Zong-Huan Ye, Zi-Hui Zhang
papers.abstract: The paper applies PPO reinforcement learning to locate detector-relevant gravitational-wave signals from supercooled phase transitions in a minimal dark U(1)x sector, comparing reward designs and PPO scans against Monte Carlo scans.

papers.title: Interpreting "Interpretability" and Explaining "Explainability" in Machine Learning in Physics
papers.authors_short: Rikab Gambhir, Luisa Lucie-Smith, Jesse Thaler
papers.abstract: The review distinguishes interpretability as structural transparency from explainability as mapping model behavior onto scientific domain knowledge, emphasizing task specification and intervention plans as core design choices for physics ML.

papers.title: CHISAO: A GPU-Native Parallel Optimizer for Multimodal Black-Box Functions via Convergence-Anticonvergence Oscillation
papers.abstract: The paper proposes a GPU-native population optimizer for recovering all modes of multimodal black-box functions, reporting complete mode recovery on standard optimization benchmarks and robustness to synthetic likelihood noise.

papers.title: Kinetic freeze-out and diffusion dynamics in small-system asymmetric collisions at sqrt(sNN)=200 GeV in light of a generalized Fokker-Planck distribution
papers.authors_short: M. Waqas, Wolfgang Bietenholz, Khusniddin K. Olimov, Muhammad Ajaz, Jihane Ben Slimane, et al.
papers.abstract: The paper fits neutral-pion pT spectra in p-Al, p-Au, d-Au, and He3-Au collisions with a generalized Fokker-Planck distribution and extracts effective temperatures, transition scales, and power-law exponents.
